source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Black and white tiles can be laid on square floors as shown in the pictures. We can see floors with 4 black and 9 black tiles respectively. In each corner there is a black tile, and each black tile touches only white tiles. How many white tiles would there be on a floor that had 25 black tiles?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the pictures', indicating the pictured layouts are needed
the target pattern depends on visual arrangement of black/white tiles on square floors, not just a standalone numeric statement
asset_registry_record shows an available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the solver must infer a tiling pattern from the pictured examples with 4 and 9 black tiles
the condition 'each black tile touches only white tiles' must be combined with the corner constraint and square-floor structure
after inferring the pattern, the solver must extrapolate from 25 black tiles to the corresponding number of white tiles
this is more than direct lookup and requires pattern induction plus counting
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='56', which is easy to compare against a computed result
the task has a single concrete output rather than an open-ended explanation
the inferred construction can be checked by counting tiles once the pattern is established
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, and asset registry passed with no listed issues
high image_dependency_score (0.95) is supported by an available primary image, so the core visual semantics are recoverable
strong verifiability_score (0.91) with a numeric answer makes downstream checking reliable
multi_step_score (0.72) indicates useful reasoning complexity without showing missing core assets
although there are risk flags about pattern extrapolation and text-only ambiguity, the required image is available, so these do not justify downgrading
